content.cn_text: 现在请仔细听并回答31至40题。
content.en_text: Now listen carefully and answer questions 31 to 40.

content.cn_text: 欢迎收听今天关于绘画修复的讲座。
content.en_text: Welcome to today's lecture about Restoration of Paintings.

content.cn_text: 我们都知道，无论一幅画作储存或处理得多么小心，它都可能受到
content.en_text: We all know that no matter how carefully a painting is stored or handled, it can be

content.cn_text: 损坏。
content.en_text: damaged.

content.cn_text: 修复工作就是修补这些损坏，但要做到这一点，并让画作恢复
content.en_text: It's the job of restoring to fix this damage, but to do so, and return a painting to the

content.cn_text: 其原始外观，我们需要了解艺术家在创作时使用的材料。
content.en_text: original appearance, we need to know about a material that artists have used when creating

content.cn_text: 它。
content.en_text: it.

content.cn_text: 因此，修复的第一阶段是确定使用了哪种颜料以及应用在哪种
content.en_text: So, the first stage through Stora is determining what kind of paint that applied to what kind

content.cn_text: 表面。
content.en_text: of surface.

content.cn_text: 修复过程还包括确定艺术品受到了何种损坏。
content.en_text: The Restoration process also involves the determines of what kind of damages have been

content.cn_text: 所造成的损害。
content.en_text: done to the artwork.

content.cn_text: 严重的损坏发生在艺术家准备悬挂和展示画作时，
content.en_text: The Arseverage Damage is concluded, when the artist is preparing to hang on and display,

content.cn_text: 他或她必须拉伸画布以使其适合画框。
content.en_text: he or she have to stretch the canvas to fit it over a frame.

content.cn_text: 这可能导致画布撕裂。
content.en_text: And this can result in the ripping of the canvas.

content.cn_text: 我们的修复师需要能够解决这类问题，并希望能从一开始就做好准备。
content.en_text: Our Stora needs to be able to fix this kind of problem and hopefully make preparation

content.cn_text: 从一开始就。
content.en_text: from the beginning.

content.cn_text: 另一件事是时间。
content.en_text: And another thing is time.

content.cn_text: 这可能对艺术品造成过度损坏，或者一年的画作可能从
content.en_text: This can be excessive damage to the art, or a year of painting can change from being

content.cn_text: 相当明亮变得暗淡（原文应为dull，此处发音有误）。
content.en_text: quite bright to appearing doubt.

content.cn_text: 这通常是由于作品表面积聚了污垢。
content.en_text: This is often due to the collection of dirt on the surface of the work.

content.cn_text: 所有这些都需要清除。
content.en_text: This all needs to be removed.

content.cn_text: 这几乎是修复师们常做的任务。
content.en_text: It was almost the common task that Stora's performed.

content.cn_text: 画作的另一个问题是颜色褪色。
content.en_text: Another problem with the paintings is fading with the colors.

content.cn_text: 如果一幅画放在不合适的地方，尤其是在阳光下晒，这会导致
content.en_text: If a painting is hardly in a round place, particularly in the tanning of the sun, this

content.cn_text: 颜料及其外观褪色。
content.en_text: fades the paint and its appearance.

content.cn_text: 这不仅发生在画作悬挂时，也发生在储存时。
content.en_text: This happens not just hanging in the painting, also in storing them.

content.cn_text: 当艺术家想要储存画作时，通常会将其卷起来储存。
content.en_text: When artists want to store the painting, it's common for them to roll it up for storage.

content.cn_text: 这个过程可能导致颜料出现裂缝。
content.en_text: This process can result in a crack in the paint.

content.cn_text: 一些艺术家，比如毕加索，可能会不同意，但颜料有可能
content.en_text: Some artists such as Picasso said he might disagree, but it's possible that the paint

content.cn_text: 完全从画布上脱落，这是任何贫困艺术家都不愿看到的。
content.en_text: could cut off from the canvas entirely, something that short broke artists wouldn't like.

content.cn_text: 当我们修复画作时，重要的是要考虑它们如何对周围环境中的事物
content.en_text: It's important that when we restoring the paintings to consider how they react to things

content.cn_text: 作出反应，例如颜料会变得发粘。
content.en_text: in your environment, for example the paint can become sticky.

content.cn_text: 这可能是一个非常严重的问题，即使是最小的
content.en_text: This can be very serious problem, which can be caused by even the minimum change of

content.cn_text: 温度变化也可能导致，这是需要注意的另一件事。
content.en_text: temperature, that this is something else to watch for.

content.cn_text: 很多艺术品损坏是由于修复师自身造成的。
content.en_text: A lot of damage to artworks because of the restorers themselves.

content.cn_text: 修复师过去常试图使画布更坚固，他们会在作品上涂抹一些东西。
content.en_text: Restorers used to try to make canvas stronger, and they put something onto the work.

content.cn_text: 其中一部分处理是将蜡涂在画布背面，但这会留下
content.en_text: Part of its processing brought the putting wax on the back of the canvas, but this leaves

content.cn_text: 画作表面可见的痕迹。
content.en_text: the visible marks to the surface of the paintings.

content.cn_text: 希望如今我们能通过科学知识，在修复中避免犯类似的
content.en_text: Hopefully, nowadays we can prove scientific knowledge, restoration can avoid making similar

content.cn_text: 错误。
content.en_text: errors.

content.cn_text: 修复师有多种暴露工具，有些是高科技的，有些则出人意料地
content.en_text: Restorers have several tools of exposures, some are high-tech, others are surprisingly

content.cn_text: 低科技。
content.en_text: low-tech.

content.cn_text: 修复师检查一幅名画时，使用某种特定工具来消除并使其停留在颜料表面之下，这并不常见，
content.en_text: It's not that common to see a restorer examine a name painting to use the certain tool

content.cn_text: 但重要的是
content.en_text: to eliminate the mind and live it under the surface of the paint, but it's important

content.cn_text: 分析艺术家使用了哪种颜料或材料。
content.en_text: to analyze what kind of paint or materials that artists are used.

content.cn_text: 修复师经常使用显微镜，这样他们可以彻底分析材料。
content.en_text: Restorers often use microscopes so they can analyze the materials thoroughly.

content.cn_text: 对于修复而言，涉及清洁画作表面，修复师使用由
content.en_text: For restoration, involves cleaning the surface of the painting, restorers use sponge made

content.cn_text: 棉花制成的海绵轻轻清洁作品。
content.en_text: from cotton to gently clean the work.

content.cn_text: 你可能好奇用什么来清洁画作，因为一些非常
content.en_text: It might be curious about what is used to clean the paintings, because some of the very

content.cn_text: 古老的画作覆盖着清漆，化学品不能用于清洁，因为
content.en_text: old paintings are covered with varnish, chemicals cannot be used for cleaning, due to the fact

content.cn_text: 它们可能会去除或损坏清漆层。
content.en_text: that they can remove a damage to the varnish layer.

content.cn_text: 但修复师必须用某种东西来清洁画作。
content.en_text: But restorers have to use something to clean the paintings.

content.cn_text: 这就是低科技工具发挥作用的地方。
content.en_text: This is where the low-tech tools come in.

content.cn_text: 清洁中最常用的液体是人的唾液。
content.en_text: The most common liquid used in cleaning is a human saliva.

content.cn_text: 它在清洁画作方面做得非常出色。
content.en_text: It does a great job of cleaning the painting.

content.cn_text: 它不会造成损坏。
content.en_text: It doesn't cause damage.

content.cn_text: 现在我将开始讨论清洁的功能。
content.en_text: Now I'll start by looking at the function of cleaning.

groups.desc: Write ONE WORD ONLY for each answer to following questions below.
groups.title: Question 31-40.

items.title: Ripping canvas was caused by over stretching of putting into a 31._______
Brightness changes due to accumulated 32______on top of painting
Paint's color fades when exposed to the light of the 33_______
Method of storage leads to the emerge of 34______in the paint
Restorers applied 36________on the back of canvas to make it stronger.
Tools such as 37______are used to examine the painting completely.
Restorers use sponges made from 38._______to clean the work softly
Old paintings were covered with varnish, 39_______can't be used for cleaning

groups.source_collect:
Write ONE WORD ONLY for each answer to following questions below.
The Restoration of Paintings
Various Damage
• Ripping canvas was caused by over stretching of putting into a $31$
• Brightness changes due to accumulated $32$on top of painting
• Paint's color fades when exposed to the light of the $33$
• Method of storage leads to the emerge of $34$in the paint
• Varied $35$causes sticky paint
Restoration Process
• Restorers applied $36$on the back of canvas to make it stronger.
• Tools such as $37$are used to examine the painting completely.
Cleaning Tools
• Restorers use sponges made from $38$to clean the work softly
• Old paintings were covered with varnish, $39$can't be used for cleaning
• The most common liquid used in cleaning is human $40$
